threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still wait for the bus even when it's clearly marked as 'Not in Service.' Everyone else just walks on.
Caught a night bus past the old biscuit factory, saw a group of ravers arguing with a seagull over a discarded kebab. Only in Bermondsey.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Last week, I watched a guy order a Döner in perfect Turkish, then debate the best vegan brunch spots for an hour with a friend. Classic Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you see people bringing their own utensils to the park for a picnic. Makes a casual stroll feel like a gourmet event.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a real local knows to avoid BART on Mondays after equipment problems. Tourists just stand confused by the delays while we know the back roads.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus with a busker playing accordion while a couple danced by the back door. Classic San Francisco, where every ride's a mini street fair.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the best tapas are in the tourist traps. The real gems are the hidden bars where you can hear locals gossip over a glass of vermut.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a recent arrival by the way they still hesitate at the unmarked crosswalks, waiting for a green light to cross.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the 'Overground' instead of the 'Ginger Line'—everyone around here knows that's the real name.
Caught a bloke trying to sell homemade perfume on the 133 last night. Smelled like a mix of cheap cologne and jaffa cakes. Never know what you'll find!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a guy playing the accordion for tips while his dog politely nodded along on the corner of Weserstraße. Classic Neukölln moment.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find a kebab shop with vegan döner that slaps harder than the classical concert next door. Pure Kreuzberg magic.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they know to avoid 16th and Mission after 9 PM—it's a whole different vibe, trust me.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tux on the 38, heading to a wedding, while the bus was packed with late-night partiers from the bar. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think Gràcia’s plaza is just for selfies, but locals know it’s our living room—where we catch up and watch the world go by.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You’ll notice them squinting at maps, often stops mid-street to take a photo of the tiniest arquitectura. It's like a walking travel guide!
